---
# =============================================================================
# blog/posts/welcome/index.qmd — Welcome post
# =============================================================================
#
# HOW TO EDIT THIS POST
# ---------------------
# • Change `title`, `description`, `date`, and `author` in the YAML header.
# • Add your own `categories` as a YAML list (e.g. [announcement, data-science]).
# • Replace `image` with a path to a cover image (place it in this folder or
#   in the top-level images/ directory and adjust the path accordingly).
# • Write your post content in Markdown below the `---` separator.
#
# HOW TO CREATE A NEW POST
# ------------------------
# 1. Create a new folder under blog/posts/, e.g. blog/posts/my-new-post/
# 2. Copy this file there and rename it index.qmd
# 3. Update the YAML front matter (title, date, etc.)
# 4. Write your content below the `---` separator
# 5. Push to GitHub — the site rebuilds automatically
#
# =============================================================================
title: "Welcome to Jules G Org"
description: >
  Introducing the Jules G Org website — what we do, how the site works,
  and what to expect from us.
author: "Jules G Org"
date: "2024-01-01"
date-modified: last-modified
categories:
  - announcement
  - quarto
image: "cover.png"          # optional cover image for the listing card
toc: true
---

## Hello, World! 👋

Welcome to the Jules G Org blog!  This is the first post on our new Quarto-
powered website, so let's use it to explain what this place is all about.

## What Is Jules G Org?

Jules G Org is a GitHub organisation that collects open-source projects
spanning data science, tooling, and technical writing.

## How This Site Is Built

The site is generated using **[Quarto](https://quarto.org)** — an open-source
scientific and technical publishing system that can render `.qmd` files
(Markdown + optional code cells) into polished HTML websites, PDFs, and more.

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "How the site is built and deployed"
%%| label: fig-deploy
flowchart LR
    A[Edit .qmd files] --> B[git push to main]
    B --> C[GitHub Actions]
    C --> D[quarto render]
    D --> E[gh-pages branch]
    E --> F[julesg-org.github.io]
```

### Editing Content

All content lives in `.qmd` files at the root of the
[julesg-org.github.org](https://github.com/julesg-org/julesg-org.github.org)
repository:

| Path | What it controls |
|------|-----------------|
| `_quarto.yml` | Site-wide config (navbar, theme, footer) |
| `index.qmd` | Home / landing page |
| `about.qmd` | About page |
| `blog/posts/<post>/index.qmd` | Individual blog posts |
| `projects/sample-project/index.qmd` | Sub-project page |
| `styles.css` | Custom CSS overrides |

## What's Next?

Look out for posts on:

- 🐍 Python and R data science workflows
- 📦 Building reusable packages
- 🔧 DevOps and CI/CD for data projects

Stay tuned, and feel free to open an issue or pull request on GitHub!
